description:
## Overview

Read or write the device's entire configuration in a single command without calling individual endpoints separately.

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `get_config` | Retrieve full configuration — version, status, region, WiFi, Ethernet, endpoints, certificates, events |
| `set_config` | Apply configuration changes — WiFi, endpoints, events, region, and more in one payload |

## Use Cases

- Bulk device configuration reads
- Fleet-wide configuration deployment
- Configuration backup and restore
- Initial device setup

post.description:
The get_config command retrieves a consolidated device configuration snapshot in a single response.

This command returns:
- Device identity, status, and regional configuration data
- Network configuration details for Wi-Fi and Ethernet
- Endpoint and event routing configuration settings
- Certificate inventory and related configuration state

Use this command to:
- Capture full-device configuration for diagnostics
- Validate baseline settings during onboarding or audits
- Compare runtime configuration across devices in a fleet

No additional command-specific payload fields are required beyond the standard request envelope fields.

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Complete Device Configuration Retrieval |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [get_version](#op-get-version), [get_status](#op-get-status), [get_current_region](#op-get-current-region), [set_config](#op-set-config) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId |
| Supported Operations | Retrieve complete device configuration snapshot |
| Supported Response Sections | currentConfig, response |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Command name for requesting complete device configuration.

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the get_config request.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 3 — Not able to retrieve information

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

Complete device config.description: Response with complete device configuration including all settings

x-error-codes.cause: The device could not gather the requested information at this time
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Retry the command after a short delay; if persistent, reboot the device

post.description:
The set_config command applies multiple configuration settings to the reader in a single request.

This command allows you to configure:
- Wi-Fi settings through wifiConfig
- Endpoint settings through epConfig
- Deferred application behavior through applyAfterReboot
- Combined network and endpoint updates in one transaction

Use this command to:
- Apply combined Wi-Fi and endpoint changes together
- Reduce multiple configuration round trips
- Control whether changes apply immediately or after reboot

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Device Configuration Management |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [get_config](#op-get-config), [set_wifi](#op-set-wifi), [config_endpoint](#op-config-endpoint), [config_events](#op-config-events) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId, configData |
| Supported Operations | CREATE and MODIFY Wi-Fi profiles; add, update, and delete endpoint configuration; apply after reboot option |
| Supported Types | Endpoint types: MGMT, MGMT_EVT, CTRL, DATA1, DATA2, SOTI, MDM; verification types: NONE, VERIFY_PEER, VERIFY_HOST, VERIFY_HOST_PEER |
| Supported Profiles | Wi-Fi access point profiles identified by ESSID with security configuration |
| Supported Protocols | MQTT, MQTT_WS, MQTT_WSS, MQTT_TLS, TCP, HTTP, HTTPS, WS, WSS, AWS, AZURE, HID |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Command name for applying device configuration changes.

requestId.description: Unique request identifier used to correlate this command with its response.

configData.title: configData
configData.description: Configuration payload containing one or more settings to apply on the device.

applyAfterReboot.description: If true, configuration changes are staged and applied after reboot.

interfaceName.description: The name of the network interface for which the Wi-Fi configuration is being set.

enableInterface.description: Specifies whether the network interface should be enabled or disabled.

operation.description: The operation to be performed on the Wi-Fi configuration. It can either create a new configuration or modify an existing one.

accessPoint.description: Configuration details of the Wi-Fi access point.

connect.description: Indicates whether to connect to this profile. If true, disconnects the currently connected profile and connects to the specified one; if false, just saves the profile.

isPreferred.description: Marks this profile as the preferred one to always connect to when available.

autoConn.description: Reserved for backward compatibility. It will be removed in the future.

essid.description: The ESSID (network name) of the access point.

enableSecurity.description: Indicates whether security is enabled for this access point.

ipAddress.description: The IPv4 address to assign to the interface.

enableDhcp.description: Specifies whether DHCP is enabled for automatic IP address assignment.

dnsServer.description: The IPv4 address of the DNS server.

domainName.description: The domain name for the network.

security.description: Security configuration for the access point.

anonymousIdentity.description: The anonymous identity for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

certificate.description: List of certificates used for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

key.description: The type of certificate to be used.

value.description: The name of the specific certificate.

WPA3Enterprise.description: WPA3-Enterprise security configuration with optional certificate parameters.

key.description: The type of certificate to be used.

value.description: The name of the specific certificate.

configuration.description: Endpoint settings used for the requested operation.

epType.description: Specifies the type of the endpoint.

protocol.description: Defines the communication protocol used by the endpoint.

activate.description: Set to true to mark this endpoint active.

url.description: Specifies the URL associated with the endpoint.

verificationType.description: Specifies the type of verification used for establishing secure connections.

port.description: Specifies the port number used for communication.

tenantId.description: Tenant identifier associated with this endpoint configuration.

mqttParams.description: MQTT-specific parameters for configuring the MQTT connection.

keepAlive.description: Specifies the keep-alive interval in seconds when client is idle.

cleanSession.description: Indicates whether to start a clean session for the MQTT client.

reconnectDelayMin.description: Specifies the minimum delay in seconds before reconnecting when a connection is lost.

reconnectDelayMax.description: Specifies the maximum delay in seconds before reconnecting when a connection is lost.

clientId.description: Specifies the client ID used for MQTT connections.

password.description: The password for authentication in MQTT connections.

topic.description: Specifies the topic to publish messages to. Supports up to 3 publish topics.

retain.description: Indicates whether the message should be retained by the broker.

ipv4Configuration.description: IPv4 settings currently applied to the Wi-Fi interface.

ipAddress.description: The IPv4 address of the device.

subnetMask.description: The subnet mask of the network.

enableDhcp.description: Indicates if DHCP is enabled for IPv4 configuration.

dnsServer.description: The IPv4 address of the DNS server.

domainName.description: The domain name of the network.

securityDetails.description: Security configuration details based on the selected Wi-Fi security type.

WPA2Personal.description: WPA2-Personal security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

password.description: The password used for WPA2-Personal security.

WPA3Personal.description: WPA3-Personal security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

password.description: The password used for WPA3-Personal security.

WPA2Enterprise.description: WPA2-Enterprise security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

innerAuthentication.description: The inner authentication protocol for WPA2-Enterprise.

identity.description: The identity used for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

anonymousIdentity.description: The anonymous identity used for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

items.description: One certificate key or value pair for WPA2-Enterprise authentication.

key.description: The type of certificate (e.g., ca_cert, client_key).

WPA3Enterprise.description: WPA3-Enterprise security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

innerAuthentication.description: The inner authentication protocol for WPA3-Enterprise.

identity.description: The identity used for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

anonymousIdentity.description: The anonymous identity used for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

items.description: One certificate key or value pair for WPA3-Enterprise authentication.

key.description: The type of certificate (e.g., ca_cert, client_key).

OWEPublic.description: OWE public-network security settings for this Wi-Fi profile.

authentication.description: The authentication method for OWE-Public access.

items.description: Wi-Fi network configuration — interface status (enabled/disabled), connected access point (SSID, signal, security type), IP addressing (IPv4, DHCP or static), and enterprise security credentials (WPA2/WPA3 Personal or Enterprise, certificates).

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 2 — Invalid payload
- 10 — Configuration already exist
- 15 — WIFI Error - SSID not found
- 17 — WIFI Error - SSID missed
- 18 — WIFI Error - SSID already exist
- 19 — WIFI Error - SSID count overflow
- 20 — Wifi is not supported
- 23 — Invalid enum value
- 25 — Max 3 publish topics exceeded
- 26 — Max 1 subscribe topic exceeded
- 27 — Invalid tenant ID length

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

x-error-codes.cause: The JSON payload is malformed or contains invalid field values
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Validate the payload against the command schema and correct any errors

x-error-codes.cause: An endpoint or configuration with the same name already exists
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Delete the existing configuration first or use a different name

x-error-codes.cause: The specified Wi-Fi SSID does not exist in saved profiles
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Verify the SSID name is correct and that the profile has been saved

x-error-codes.cause: The required SSID field is missing from the payload
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Include the SSID field in the command payload

x-error-codes.cause: A Wi-Fi profile with this SSID is already saved on the device
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Delete the existing profile first or update it instead of creating a new one

x-error-codes.cause: The maximum number of saved Wi-Fi profiles has been reached
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Delete an unused Wi-Fi profile before adding a new one

x-error-codes.cause: The device hardware does not have a Wi-Fi interface
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Use Ethernet or another supported interface for network connectivity

x-error-codes.cause: A field value does not match any of the allowed enum options
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the schema for allowed values and correct the field

x-error-codes.cause: More than 3 publish topics were specified in the endpoint configuration
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Reduce the number of publish topics to 3 or fewer

x-error-codes.cause: More than 1 subscribe topic was specified in the endpoint configuration
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Use only 1 subscribe topic per endpoint

x-error-codes.cause: The tenant ID exceeds the maximum allowed character length
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Shorten the tenant ID to within the allowed limit
